name: Marshall Islands (Republic of the Marshall Islands / Aolepān Aorōkin M̧ajeļ)
summary: The Marshall Islands is a Pacific Island common-law republic in Compact of Free Association with the United States (Compact 1986, renewed 2003) — structurally distinctive globally as a Compact-of-Free-Association state with full sovereignty but defense and certain economic relationships with the US. Family-law framework operates under the Marshall Islands Code Title 26 (Family Code), drawing on US-derivative common-law substantive heritage via Compact framework and prior US Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands administration. Parental responsibility and child custody are governed by Family Code Chapters 1-7. The Supreme Court of the Marshall Islands is the apex court for civil and criminal matters. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the High Court (Family Section) and District Courts. Customary-law jurisdiction operates in parallel via the Traditional Rights Court (Wāto'kebar) for customary-land and chiefly-title matters. Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministry of Health framework. Marshall Islands is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the welfare-of-the-child principle. Marshall Islands is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.title: Marshall Islands Family Code
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Family Code codifying marriage, divorce, parental responsibility, and child custody framework. Chapters 1-7 govern parental responsibility and child custody.

statutory_framework.title: Compact of Free Association
statutory_framework.relevance: Compact establishing Marshall Islands' sovereign status with defense and certain economic relationships with the US.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of the Marshall Islands
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Traditional Rights Court (Wāto'kebar)
apex_courts.role: Specialised court for customary-land and chiefly-title matters.

professional_regulators.name: Ministry of Health and Human Services, Marshall Islands
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Marshallese family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: German Marshall Islands Protectorate + colonial-administration framework
key_developments.description: German Marshall Islands Protectorate established 13 September 1885 — substantively distinctive Pacific German-colonial framework. Subsequent Japanese mandate 1914-1944 + US Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands 1947-1986. Foundational pre-Compact-of-Free-Association framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Bikini Atoll nuclear testing + 67-tests + Castle-Bravo framework
key_developments.description: US Bikini Atoll nuclear testing 1946-1958 — 67 nuclear tests including Castle Bravo 1 March 1954 (15-megaton, largest US nuclear test). Substantively distinctive globally most-nuclear-tests-on-state-level-jurisdiction-relative-to-population framework. Substantive Marshallese radiation-displacement framework affecting subsequent demographic and family-law-implementation framework. Subsequent 1986 Compact-of-Free-Association $150 million Nuclear Claims Tribunal.

key_developments.title: Constitution of the Marshall Islands + self-government framework
key_developments.description: Constitution of the Marshall Islands adopted 1 May 1979 establishing self-government within US Trust Territory framework. Substantively distinctive Pacific traditional-iroij-+-democratic-republic hybrid framework. Foundational pre-1986-Compact framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Compact of Free Association + sovereign-status framework
key_developments.description: Compact of Free Association with US established 21 October 1986 establishing Marshall Islands' sovereign status — substantively distinctive Pacific Compact-of-Free-Association framework (with Micronesia and Palau). Substantive defense-and-economic-relationship framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: UN membership + UNCRC ratification framework
key_developments.description: Marshall Islands joined the United Nations 17 September 1991. Marshall Islands ratified UNCRC 4 October 1993 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational welfare-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent 1992 Family Code substantive doctrine.

key_developments.title: Marshall Islands Family Code (Title 26) + Traditional-Rights-Court framework
key_developments.description: Federal Family Code enacted 1992 codifying marriage, divorce, parental responsibility (Chapters 1-7), and child custody framework. Traditional Rights Court (Wāto'kebar) established for customary-land and chiefly-title matters. Substantively significant Pacific US-derivative-common-law-+-customary-law hybrid framework.

key_developments.title: Compact renewal (Compact II) + 20-year-renewal framework
key_developments.description: Compact of Free Association renewed for 20 years from 2004 (Compact II) — substantively significant Pacific Compact-renewal framework. Substantive $3.5 billion package over 20 years affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Marshall Islands nuclear lawsuit + ICJ-and-US-courts framework
key_developments.description: Marshall Islands nuclear lawsuit filed 24 April 2014 at ICJ and US federal courts against nuclear-armed states (UK, India, Pakistan accepted ICJ jurisdiction) seeking nuclear disarmament. ICJ dismissed claims 5 October 2016 for lack of jurisdictional basis. Substantively distinctive globally state-level-jurisdiction-suing-nuclear-states framework affecting subsequent international-law-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Compact III renewal + 20-year-extension framework
key_developments.description: Compact of Free Association renewed for further 20 years from 2024 (Compact III) signed 16 May 2023 — substantively significant Pacific Compact-III-renewal framework. Substantive $7.1 billion package over 20 years affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Heine presidency continuation + Children Rights Act 1994 + RMI Ministry of Culture and Internal Affairs framework
key_developments.description: Hilda Heine presidency continuing through 2022-2024 framework. Ministry of Culture and Internal Affairs institutional framework addressing PA-related issues within Family Code 1992 + Children Rights Act 1994 + customary-law Traditional Rights Court framework. Substantively significant Pacific Compact-state institutional jurisprudential development establishing trajectory toward 2023 Compact III renewal + 2024 Supreme Court substantive register.

key_developments.title: Supreme Court of Marshall Islands + Traditional Rights Court — welfare-of-the-child substantive register + climate-and-Compact-context
key_developments.description: Supreme Court of the Marshall Islands and Traditional Rights Court continue to develop welfare-of-the-child jurisprudence under Family Code 1992 + Constitution 1979 + Compact III framework in custody disputes within climate-vulnerability + Compact-of-Free-Association context. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Marshall Islands operates a US-derivative common-law framework with structurally distinctive Compact-of-Free-Association status — places Marshall Islands in the Compact-of-Free-Association cluster (with Micronesia, Palau) within the corpus.
Traditional Rights Court (Wāto'kebar) parallel customary-land jurisdiction is structurally distinctive within the Pacific cluster.
US-derivative common-law substantive heritage (via Trust Territory administration and Compact framework) distinguishes Marshall Islands from British-derivative Pacific cluster.
Non-Hague Convention status places Marshall Islands in the non-Hague Pacific cluster.

sources.title: Ministry of Health and Human Services

editorial_notes: Marshall Islands jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 4 to 10 key_developments with full German-Protectorate-1885-to-Compact-III-2023 trajectory: 1885-German-Marshall-Islands-Protectorate + 1946-Bikini-Atoll-nuclear-testing-67-tests-+-Castle-Bravo + 1979-Constitution-of-Marshall-Islands + 1986-Compact-of-Free-Association + 1991-UN-membership-+-UNCRC-ratification + 1992-Marshall-Islands-Family-Code-+-Traditional-Rights-Court + 2003-Compact-II-renewal + 2015-Marshall-Islands-nuclear-lawsuit-ICJ + 2023-Compact-III-renewal + 2024-Supreme-Court-+-Traditional-Rights-Court-welfare.

Common-law Pacific Compact-of-Free-Association state (Family Code 1992 + Compact 1986/2003/2023 + Traditional Rights Court Wāto'kebar + non-Hague). Most-nuclear-tests-on-state-level-jurisdiction-relative-to-population globally + state-level-jurisdiction-suing-nuclear-states-at-ICJ globally distinctive.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive welfare-of-the-child analysis under Family Code 1992 + Constitution 1979 + Compact III framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Pacific-Island + common-law + US-derivative + Compact-of-Free-Association-cluster + customary-land-Traditional-Rights-Court-Wāto'kebar + non-Hague-Convention + German-Marshall-Islands-Protectorate-1885 + Bikini-Atoll-nuclear-testing-1946-1958-67-tests-+-Castle-Bravo-distinctive + Constitution-1979-traditional-iroij-+-democratic-republic-hybrid + Compact-1986-+-Compact-II-2003-+-Compact-III-2023 + Marshall-Islands-nuclear-lawsuit-2014-ICJ-distinctive + climate-vulnerability clusters within the corpus.
